publish_state_policy.ranking_scope: default ranked outputs use the active enabled workshop portfolio only

stale_policy.definition: Any content older than 12 months is treated as stale.

top_performer_formula.score_meaning: Higher is better. A higher score means stronger demand plus fresher content inside the same workshop or sprint population.

variables.name: recent_views_12m_content_percentile
variables.meaning: Long-run demand compared with the same content type.

variables.name: recent_views_90d_content_percentile
variables.meaning: Recent demand compared with the same content type.

variables.name: freshness_score
variables.meaning: Reward for content updated in the last 12 months. Older content gets a smaller boost.

retire_score_formula.score_meaning: Higher is worse. A higher score means the content item should be reviewed sooner for refresh or retirement.

variables.name: demand_pressure
variables.meaning: Mostly 12-month demand, with a smaller 90-day demand check so short-term drops also count.

variables.name: staleness_score
variables.meaning: How old the last meaningful content update is. Anything older than 12 months is stale.

variables.name: replacement_support_score
variables.meaning: How strong the replacement path is. Confirmed replacements count more than review-only replacements.

variables.name: no_owner_score
variables.meaning: Extra pressure when nobody clearly owns the workshop.

low_demand_thresholds.rule: views_12m_percentile <= 10, with extra pressure if recent_views_90d < 25

replacement_formula.score_meaning: Higher is better. A higher score means the candidate is a stronger replacement match.
availability_rules: Candidates without current content or repo evidence are excluded.
Only candidates from the same content type are compared.
content_source_order: actual markdown content from live GitHub when available
actual markdown content from the local repo mirror
metadata-only fallback when content could not be loaded

date_sync_policy.green: Dates match at the file precision shown in the acknowledgement section.
date_sync_policy.yellow: Dates differ, but by 12 months or less.
date_sync_policy.red: Dates differ by more than 12 months.

top_to_retire_rules.description: retire-now content item: high retirement score, confirmed replacement, very low demand, updated more than 24 months ago, and published more than 30 months ago
first retire-now fallback: lower the score bar slightly but still require confirmed replacement plus the strict stale and age gates
second retire-now fallback: allow confirmed or review replacement, keep a high score, and relax stale age to 18 months
third retire-now fallback: broaden low-demand slightly, allow confirmed or review replacement, and require stale content older than 12 months
fourth retire-now fallback: keep retirement pressure visible with stale content older than 12 months, broader low-demand thresholds, and confirmed or review replacement
final retire-now filler: include stale low-demand content older than 12 months even when replacement still needs human confirmation

top_retire_candidate_rules.description: high-risk content item: strong retirement score, low demand, replacement path, and content older than 18 months
at-risk content item: strong score, low demand, replacement path, and stale content older than 12 months
moderate-risk content item: moderate score, low demand, replacement path, and stale content older than 12 months
watchlist content item: include stale low-demand content older than 12 months for human review even if replacement is not settled yet
